description: Ferni-voiced phrases for Anxiety domain - Better-Than-Human calm in the storm

immediate: <break time="350ms"/>Feel your feet on the ground. <break time="300ms"/>Feel the chair beneath you. <break time="250ms"/>You're here. <break time="200ms"/>Right now.
<break time="400ms"/>Name five things you can see. <break time="350ms"/>Go slow. <break time="300ms"/>I'll wait.
returning: <break time="350ms"/>Good. <break time="300ms"/>You're coming back. <break time="250ms"/>Keep breathing.
<break time="400ms"/>There you are. <break time="350ms"/>You found your way back.
<break time="350ms"/>The ground is still here. <break time="300ms"/>It always is.

acknowledging: <break time="400ms"/>Anxiety lies. <break time="350ms"/>It tells you everything is dangerous. <break time="300ms"/>But you're safe right now.
<break time="350ms"/>Your nervous system is trying to protect you. <break time="300ms"/>It's just... overzealous.
<break time="400ms"/>The fear feels real. <break time="350ms"/>The danger isn't. <break time="300ms"/>Let's separate those.
normalizing: <break time="350ms"/>Millions of people feel exactly this way. <break time="300ms"/>You're not broken.
<break time="400ms"/>Anxiety is exhausting. <break time="350ms"/>Of course you're tired. <break time="300ms"/>You've been fighting all day.
<break time="350ms"/>This doesn't mean you're weak. <break time="300ms"/>It means your alarm system is sensitive.

interrupting: <break time="400ms"/>Pause. <break time="350ms"/>Where did the spiral start? <break time="300ms"/>Let's trace it back.
<break time="350ms"/>Your mind went three steps ahead. <break time="300ms"/>Let's come back to this step.
<break time="400ms"/>That's future-tripping. <break time="350ms"/>The future isn't here yet. <break time="300ms"/>Only now is.
reality_testing: <break time="350ms"/>What's the actual evidence? <break time="300ms"/>Not the feeling. <break time="250ms"/>The facts.
<break time="400ms"/>Has this specific fear ever come true before? <break time="350ms"/>What actually happened?
<break time="350ms"/>What would you tell a friend who had this worry? <break time="300ms"/>Be that friend to yourself.

acknowledging: <break time="400ms"/>Your body is speaking. <break time="350ms"/>The racing heart. <break time="300ms"/>The tight chest. <break time="250ms"/>It's trying to help.
<break time="350ms"/>Those physical feelings are real. <break time="300ms"/>They're just not accurate.
<break time="400ms"/>Your body thinks there's danger. <break time="350ms"/>We need to show it there isn't.
calming: <break time="350ms"/>Long exhale. <break time="300ms"/>Longer than the inhale. <break time="250ms"/>That signals safety to your nervous system.
<break time="400ms"/>Put your hand on your chest. <break time="350ms"/>Feel your heart. <break time="300ms"/>It's working for you.
<break time="350ms"/>Cold water on your wrists. <break time="300ms"/>Or your face. <break time="250ms"/>It resets the system.

before_event: <break time="400ms"/>You're anxious about something that hasn't happened yet. <break time="350ms"/>You've survived the buildup before.
<break time="350ms"/>The anticipation is usually worse than the thing. <break time="300ms"/>Remember that.
<break time="400ms"/>What's the minimum viable preparation? <break time="350ms"/>Do that. <break time="300ms"/>Then stop.
uncertainty: <break time="350ms"/>You can't know what will happen. <break time="300ms"/>Nobody can. <break time="250ms"/>But you can handle whatever comes.
<break time="400ms"/>Uncertainty isn't dangerous. <break time="350ms"/>It's just uncomfortable.
<break time="350ms"/>You've navigated uncertainty before. <break time="300ms"/>You're still here.

three_am: <break time="400ms"/>Everything feels worse at night. <break time="350ms"/>That's real. <break time="300ms"/>But it's temporary.
<break time="350ms"/>Your brain is tired. <break time="300ms"/>It's not thinking clearly. <break time="250ms"/>Don't trust the 3 AM thoughts.
<break time="400ms"/>The morning will feel different. <break time="350ms"/>It always does. <break time="300ms"/>Let's just get you there.
cant_sleep: <break time="350ms"/>Don't fight it. <break time="300ms"/>Rest without sleeping is still rest.
<break time="400ms"/>Your only job right now is to lie here. <break time="350ms"/>That's it. <break time="300ms"/>Sleep will come.
<break time="350ms"/>Name the thoughts. <break time="300ms"/>Don't argue with them. <break time="250ms"/>Just name them and let them pass.

anxiety_truths: <break time="400ms"/>Anxiety wants you to fight or flee. <break time="350ms"/>The third option is to stay. <break time="300ms"/>And breathe.
<break time="350ms"/>You've survived every anxious moment so far. <break time="300ms"/>That's a 100% success rate.
<break time="400ms"/>Anxiety is uncomfortable. <break time="350ms"/>It's not dangerous. <break time="300ms"/>You can feel it and still be okay.
control: <break time="350ms"/>You can't control the anxiety. <break time="300ms"/>You can control your response to it.
<break time="400ms"/>Let the anxious thought be there. <break time="350ms"/>Don't push it away. <break time="300ms"/>Just don't follow it.
<break time="350ms"/>Acceptance isn't defeat. <break time="300ms"/>It's the first step out.
